timeline.event: Deploy agent-runtime v2.3.1 (async completion reporting optimization)
T-2001 reported: task reports completed before writing artifact
On-call engineer Alex picks up alert
T-2002 reported: 12 tasks completed, only 4 have artifacts
T-2003 reported: Pilot Bank A escalation (8 empty reports)
Alerts cleared: both metrics return to baseline

confirmed_facts: Agent-runtime v2.3.1 deployed at 09:15 UTC on 2025-06-12 (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
The v2.3.1 change included 'optimized async task completion reporting pipeline' and 'updated status transition logic' (deployment-log.md)
completed_without_artifact metric rose from 0.2/min baseline to peak 6.2/min at 10:00-10:30 (system-metrics.txt, team-notes.md)
Pilot Bank A identified 8 empty compliance reports (customer-email.txt, tickets.json T-2003)
Hotfix v2.3.2 deployed at 11:30 UTC (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
Alerts cleared at 11:32 UTC; metrics returned to baseline post-hotfix (system-metrics.txt, deployment-log.md)
T-2004 (dashboard UI) is a separate low-severity ticket unrelated to the runtime incident (tickets.json — different area)
Dashboard UI V2 Redesign is a separate project explicitly unrelated to INC-2025-0612 (team-notes.md)
likely_root_cause: Agent-runtime v2.3.1's async completion reporting optimization changed the status transition order to report 'completed' before confirming artifact writes, without adding verification that the async write succeeded.
supporting_evidence: System metrics anomaly begins within 15 minutes of v2.3.1 deployment (system-metrics.txt vs deployment-log.md)
Engineer Alex identified the exact mechanism at 10:30 (team-notes.md): 'reports complete first, then writes asynchronously — but there's no check that the async write actually succeeded'
Hotfix v2.3.2 rolled back the change and resolved the issue within 2 minutes (deployment-log.md, system-metrics.txt)
Ticket cluster T-2001, T-2002, T-2003 all describe the same core symptom with escalating severity (tickets.json)
inferences: Approximately 280-320 tasks were affected during the ~2-hour incident window (6.2/min peak over ~50 min anomaly)
The canary smoke tests did not cover the artifact-write failure path, as they passed despite the bug
The customer's 'since around 08:00' claim is an estimate that conflates observation start time with anomaly start time — the metrics confirm the bug began at 09:15
The 4-of-8 rolling deployment strategy may have partially masked the anomaly during deployment rollout
unknowns: Exact number of affected tasks and which customer accounts beyond Pilot Bank A were impacted
Whether downstream audit pipelines ingested empty reports beyond those caught by Pilot Bank A's manual review
The precise pre-v2.3.0 write-ack code state — v2.3.0 (2025-06-08) may have already weakened the guarantee before v2.3.1 broke it entirely
SLA breach determination requires total task volume data not present in the provided files
Why the SRE runbook for write-confirmation alerts was still pending update (mentioned in team-notes.md)
recommended_actions: Add post-write verification to the task completion pipeline — report 'failed' with explicit error if artifact write does not succeed (prevents recurrence regardless of status-ordering strategy)
Add integration tests for the completed-without-artifact negative path: simulate write failures, timeouts, and storage latency (fills the test gap that allowed the bug to reach production)

Create automated deployment rollback runbook: correlate 'completed_without_artifact' alerts with recent runtime deployments and provide one-click rollback (reduces incident window from 2h15m to minutes)
information_gaps: Task-level logs showing individual write attempts and failures
Total task volume for Pilot Bank A to determine SLA compliance
SRE post-mortem on the tool-call write path
Complete customer impact scope beyond Pilot Bank A
